skill.name: qms-audit-expert
skill.description: QMS audit guidance for ISO 13485 quality management systems, including audit planning, execution, nonconformity review, and CAPA verification.
skill.summary: Plan and review ISO 13485 QMS audits for medical device quality teams.
skill.author: alirezarezvani

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, environment access, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Manual review found these are false positives from markdown formatting, placeholder documentation, ASCII diagrams, and an evaluation JSON file; no executable command construction, environment secret access, network behavior, or prompt injection was found.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: Verdict: false positive. The flagged Ruby or shell backtick locations are markdown code fences, inline filename formatting, ASCII tree diagrams, or JSON strings. No shell command construction or execution path was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirmed the flagged backticks are documentation markup or serialized review text. No executable code uses these locations as shell input.

low_findings.title: Static Environment Access Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: Verdict: false positive. The flagged configuration-library locations are YAML frontmatter, compliance prose, or JSON content. No environment variable or secret access was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain metadata, headings, bullet text, and report examples. They do not call environment APIs or load configuration files.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: Verdict: false positive. The flagged weak-cryptography locations are ISO audit terminology, placeholder asset references, API documentation headings, and serialized report prose. No cryptographic code was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is non-executable documentation or JSON text. There are no hash, cipher, signature, or password-handling operations at the cited locations.

low_findings.title: Static Reconnaissance Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: Verdict: false positive. The flagged reconnaissance locations refer to audit activities such as software audits and best-practice guidance. No network scanning or host discovery behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are domain prose about quality system audits. They contain no networking commands, target enumeration, or discovery logic.

content.user_title: Audit QMS Compliance with ISO 13485 Guidance
content.value_statement: Medical device teams need structured audit planning and defensible findings. This skill helps prepare ISO 13485 audits, classify nonconformities, and plan CAPA verification.
actual_capabilities: Build risk-based internal audit schedules for ISO 13485 QMS processes.
Create audit scopes, criteria, questions, and evidence collection plans.
Classify audit findings as major nonconformities, minor nonconformities, observations, or best practices.
Connect audit findings to CAPA initiation, verification, and effectiveness checks.
Prepare teams for certification, customer, and regulatory audit scenarios.
Review audit program metrics and identify improvement opportunities.
limitations: It does not replace a qualified lead auditor or regulatory counsel.
It does not confirm current regulatory changes without user-provided sources.
It cannot certify compliance or approve a quality management system.
It relies on accurate process, record, and procedure details from the user.

use_cases.title: Plan Annual Internal Audits
use_cases.description: Create a risk-based audit program that covers critical QMS processes and prior findings.

use_cases.title: Prepare Audit Checklists
use_cases.description: Turn ISO 13485 clauses and procedure requirements into focused audit questions and evidence requests.

use_cases.title: Prepare for External Audits
use_cases.description: Run readiness reviews for certification, customer, or regulatory audits before the audit date.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Audit Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for one QMS process
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a simple ISO 13485 internal audit plan for our purchasing process. Include scope, criteria, audit questions, records to sample, and expected outputs.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Risk-Based Schedule
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me build an annual risk-based audit schedule for design control, purchasing, production, CAPA, document control, training, and post-market surveillance.

prompt_templates.title: Classify Audit Findings
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these audit observations and classify each as major nonconformity, minor nonconformity, observation, or best practice. Explain the rationale and required evidence.

prompt_templates.title: Design CAPA Verification
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a follow-up audit plan to verify CAPA effectiveness for repeated training record gaps. Include sampling logic, interview targets, records, and closure criteria.

output_examples.input: Create an audit plan for ISO 13485 design controls.
output: Scope covers design planning, inputs, outputs, review, verification, validation, transfer, and changes.
Evidence includes design plans, design history files, verification protocols, validation reports, and change records.
Key questions test traceability, approval control, risk linkage, and objective evidence of design transfer.

output_examples.input: Classify a finding where training records are missing for two operators.
output: Likely classification is minor nonconformity if the issue is isolated and process controls remain effective.
Escalate to major nonconformity if records show a systemic training process failure.
Request sampled training matrices, procedure revision history, and supervisor interview evidence.

output_examples.input: Prepare for a certification audit next month.
output: Complete open internal audit actions and confirm CAPA effectiveness evidence.
Review document control, management review, supplier controls, complaint handling, and post-market surveillance records.
Run a mock opening meeting and prepare process owners for evidence-based answers.

best_practices: Provide the current procedure, clause, process owner, and audit objective before asking for an audit plan.
Ask for findings to include requirement, objective evidence, classification, and recommended follow-up.
Use sampled records and traceability checks to verify process effectiveness, not only procedure existence.
anti_patterns: Do not treat generated audit questions as a complete regulatory checklist without local review.
Do not classify findings without objective evidence and the applicable requirement.
Do not use the skill to certify compliance or replace independent auditor judgment.

faq.question: Does this skill perform a real audit?
faq.answer: No. It helps plan, structure, and review audit work. A qualified auditor must perform and approve the audit.

faq.question: Which standard does it focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on ISO 13485 quality management systems for medical device organizations.

faq.question: Can it classify nonconformities?
faq.answer: Yes. It can suggest classifications when you provide the requirement, evidence, process context, and impact.

faq.question: Can it prepare external audit responses?
faq.answer: Yes. It can organize readiness checks, evidence requests, mock audit questions, and response planning.

faq.question: Does it check current regulations automatically?
faq.answer: No. Provide current regulatory sources or ask your AI tool to verify current requirements separately.

faq.question: Is it useful outside medical devices?
faq.answer: Some audit methods are general, but the guidance is written for ISO 13485 and medical device QMS contexts.
